qa_pairs.question: What are the steps to create a local Python wheel from source code after making modifications, assuming the source is cloned from a remote repository?
qa_pairs.answer: To create a local Python wheel from modified source code, first clone the repository (e.g., `git clone https://git.example.com/MyProject.git`), then navigate into its directory (`cd MyProject`). Check out a specific commit ID (`git checkout <commit id>`) and make any necessary edits. Finally, with your virtual environment activated, run `pip wheel --no-deps -w $HOME .` to build the wheel in your home directory.

qa_pairs.question: After creating a local Python wheel, how can you install it into your active virtual environment?
qa_pairs.answer: Once a local Python wheel has been created, you can install it into your activated virtual environment using the command `pip install $HOME/MyProject-1.0.0-py3-none.whl`. You would replace `$HOME/MyProject-1.0.0-py3-none.whl` with the actual path and filename of the wheel you generated.
